content: Lung cancer diagnosis requires tissue confirmation and comprehensive staging. Initial evaluation: chest CT with contrast, PET-CT for staging (detects distant metastases), brain MRI (especially for adenocarcinoma, SCLC, stage III+). Tissue acquisition: bronchoscopy with biopsy for central lesions; CT-guided needle biopsy for peripheral lesions; EBUS-TBNA for mediastinal lymph node sampling; thoracentesis if pleural effusion present. Pathology: histologic subtype classification; immunohistochemistry (TTF-1 for adenocarcinoma, P40/P63 for squamous); neuroendocrine markers for SCLC. Molecular testing: NGS panel preferred to detect multiple alterations simultaneously; liquid biopsy (ctDNA) if tissue inadequate or for monitoring. Staging: TNM system 8th edition; SCLC traditionally staged as limited (confined to one radiation field) vs extensive. Pulmonary function tests assess surgical candidacy. Performance status (ECOG) guides treatment tolerance. Surveillance post-treatment: CT chest every 6 months for 2 years, then annually.
